question: Will Donald Trump issue an executive order imposing new tariffs on EU-origin goods by June 14, 2026?
reason: Recent trade agreements and tariff reductions between the U.S.
evidence: On May 20, 2026, the European Union finalized a trade agreement with the United States, eliminating tariffs on U.S. industrial goods and granting preferential access to U.S. agricultural products. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/economic-indicators/eu-paves-way-to-finalise-us-trade-deal-and-avoid-trump-tariff-hike-4700151?utm_source=openai))

On May 7, 2026, President Trump gave the EU until July 4 to implement the trade deal, warning of higher tariffs if not fulfilled. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/may/07/trump-gives-eu-until-4-july-to-ratify-trade-deal-or-face-much-higher-tariffs?utm_source=openai))

The U.S. Supreme Court ruled on February 20, 2026, that President Trump cannot impose tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, leading to the withdrawal of previous tariffs. ([cfr.org](https://www.cfr.org/articles/tracking-trumps-trade-deals//?utm_source=openai))
searchQueries: Donald Trump executive order tariffs EU May June 2026
